programme.title: Cold War Armageddon: Paranoia
programme.desc: As the propaganda war and the arms race rage on, the 1950's becomes the decade of paranoia. The stalemate between democracy and communism reaches a fever pitch as the world descends further in chaos.

programme.title: The Civil War: Brothers Divided: Battle of Bull Run
programme.desc: In what will be the first real battle of the Civil War, untested and poorly trained Rebel and Confederate troops clash on the blood soaked banks of the Bull Run River.

programme.title: The Civil War: Brothers Divided: Battle of Antietam
programme.desc: Following a string of victories in Virginia, Confederate military genius General Robert E. Lee brings his army north in a bold attempt to end the Civil War and win Southern Independence.

programme.title: The Civil War: Brothers Divided: Battle of Fredericksburg
programme.desc: Following the strategic Union win at Antietam, federal forces clash with a massive Rebel army in the town of Fredericksburg, Virginia in what will become the largest fight of the Civil War.

programme.title: The Civil War: Brothers Divided: Battle of Gettysburg
programme.desc: An elite band of Yankee Sharpshooters must stop Robert E. Lee's Alabamian shock troops from flanking the Union line and winning the battle of Gettysburg and perhaps the entire Civil War.

programme.title: The Civil War: Brothers Divided: Battle of Nashville
programme.desc: In a daring attempt to regain the upper hand in the Civil War, Confederate forces try to retake the city of Nashville and sever the supply line that's feeding the Union invasion of the south.

programme.title: The Civil War: Brothers Divided: Battle of Petersburg
programme.desc: The Union invasion of the south has ground to a halt at the seemingly impregnable ring of trenches built around Petersburg, Virginia. After eight bloody attempts to storm them have failed, two officers from Vermont will lead a daring raid.

programme.title: Cold War Armageddon: Warmonger
programme.desc: In the early 1980's, the next battlefield in the struggle between communism and democracy emerges, and it's in the United State's own backyard.

programme.title: Cold War Armageddon: Cold War II
programme.desc: By the end of the 70's, war between East and West has been raging for decades. Countless proxy wars have proven how destructive the Cold War can be. It's an arms race, a propaganda battle and at times a deadly game.

programme.title: Cold War Armageddon: Peace and War
programme.desc: By the mid 1970s, the Cold War has remained a fact of life for decades. But 30 years into the war, and the doctrine of mutually assured destruction doesn't feel like a roadmap to peace. It's an arms race, a propaganda and at times, a deadly game.

programme.title: The Cowboy: Hollywood vs. History
programme.desc: The story of the Old West as it was and as it was portrayed by the giants of cinema. Legendary lawmen like Wyatt Earp and 'Wild Bill' Hickok, infamous outlaws like Jesse James and Billy the Kid, and epic showdowns like the Gunfight at the OK Corral.

programme.desc: Ashley and Michael see hidden potential in a fire-damaged Victorian and decide to save it from demolition. They open up the cramped space by turning a breezeway into a kitchen and repurpose an old safe.

programme.title: Big Texas Fix: The Pink Shack
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley have their work cut out for them while restoring a dilapidated 1914 fixer-upper. They create a light-filled breakfast nook and repurpose a vintage wooden sign to transform a quaint pink corner house.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: The Honeymoon Suite
programme.desc: In a Maine Cabin Masters first, Chase and his creative crew help a newlywed couple take a questionable 'wedding gift' and turn it into an alpine ski bungalow that would rival those found in the Swiss Alps.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Inspirations Brewing
programme.desc: When Chase and his brother-in-law learn that the owner of their latest cabin project works at Allagash Brewing Company, they're excited to check out the brewery for inspiration as they turn the weekend getaway into a three-season home away from home.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: It Takes a Village
programme.desc: Founded in 1935, Agassiz Village has provided underprivileged and at-risk kids an opportunity to experience camp. Chase and his team take on a big project with a small budget to help the camp with the support of the community.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: City Slickers Off the Grid
programme.desc: Chase and his team of builders renovate an off-the-grid camp for a family of city slickers. With limited resources, Chase and his team work to incorporate the comforts of city living into the camp.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Six Weeks For Two A-Frames
programme.desc: Chase and his team are called in to renovate two A-Frames in Sugarloaf Valley. With only six weeks to accomplish both renovations, Chase splits the team down the middle. A friendly wager allows the winning team to dress the losing team up at dinner.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Yard Crashers: Modern Zen Backyard
programme.desc: Emily and Bryan have a barren yard with an existing shed so, instead of fixing it, Matt and his crew make it a feature. They build floating decks around reflecting pools and install a new pergola to create shade, transforming the space into an oasis.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Containables: Deadlines and Due Dates
programme.desc: Jon and Kristen design a three-container structure in the Smoky Mountains of North Carolina. Dealing with tricky terrain and inclement mountain weather, the team enlists their families' help to install bowling alley wood countertops, patio doors and more.

programme.title: Containables: Containers of Staggering Size
programme.desc: Jon and Kristen want to change the way people think about container living and start by tackling their biggest build yet. They stagger two 40-foot containers and open the walls between them to create a massive great room.

programme.title: Containables: Hurricanes and Golf Balls
programme.desc: Jon and Kristen design and build a container home for a local couple's adopted son and fill it with fun features like a lofted bed, hydraulic TV winch and an outdoor fire pit. A second container has a custom workshop and a rooftop driving range.

programme.title: Containables: A Great Room With Great Views
programme.desc: Jon and Kristen raise the bar by taking on their biggest container home yet for a homeowner who wants a home where her entire family can bunk down and enjoy the amazing views her property affords.

programme.title: Containables: Stacking the Decks to a T
programme.desc: Jon and Kristen are eager to begin work on a very special container home that combines a unique design with the scenic beauty of South Texas. The home consists of three containers stacked to create a cantilevered look with multi-level decks.

programme.title: Containables: Container Home Away From Home
programme.desc: Jon and Kristen work to build a container home for the in-laws when they come to visit their new grandson. With only four weeks to complete the project, the couple must must tackle 20-foot and 40-foot containers.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Scrap Wood Basement
programme.desc: Nicole takes on a basement restoration in order to help out a sick friend and does her best to use as much scrap wood and materials as she possible can.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Too Close for Comfort
programme.desc: Nicole finds her home in Minnesota to be flooded from burst pipes due to the extreme cold. She and her crew try to discover where all they leak are located in the house.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: A Surprise for Tessa
programme.desc: Nicole's doing a surprise home renovation for one of her carpenters, Bobby, and his family. Bobby and Karen started renovating their house two years ago, but their daughter was diagnosed with cancer and they turned their focus to her.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: A Surprise for Tessa
programme.desc: Tessa and her family are coming home soon, and Nicole needs to finish their surprise home renovation before they do. She tackles the laundry room and hallway in the back addition of Bobby and Karen's house.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Front Porch Frenzy
programme.desc: Adam and Rachael hire Nicole to fully restore their newly purchased 1920s house. The couple is getting married and need to move into the home in a month, so Nicole must work faster than ever to complete everything on time.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Living Room Overhaul
programme.desc: Continuing her restoration of Adam and Rachael's home, Nicole moves her attention to the living room, where she must restore the beat up hardwood floors.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Tile Terrors
programme.desc: Turning her attention to Adam and Rachael's bathroom, Nicole deals with the many problems that creep up while working with tile in an old house. When she hits a snag, she must figure out a way to make her fixtures fit in the small space.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Kitchen Countdown
programme.desc: Nicole must finish a kitchen renovation while newlyweds are on their honeymoon.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Stepping Up the Living Room
programme.desc: Nicole turns her attention to a rundown cottage in need of major rejuvenation in Lake Orion, Michigan. Starting with the living room, she rips out an old staircase and uses an old door to tie the rooms together, making the space look grand.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Laundering Around
programme.desc: Nicole is challenged to fix the back extension of the rundown cottage in Lake Orion, Michigan. The space is too small to be a garage, so she comes up with a plan to fit laundry, a bathroom and an inviting entryway into this small area.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: All Hands On Shed
programme.desc: The backyard of the Lake Orion, Michigan, cottage is underutilized, so Nicole Curtis decides to turn this eyesore into a peaceful hangout.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Reshaping the Master Bath
programme.desc: With a limited amount of space in the Lake Orion house, Nicole Curtis decides that the best use of the second upstairs bedroom is to transform it into a master bathroom.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Dates on a Plane
programme.desc: Noah Cappe arrives in the California desert for a date with deliciousness at the Riverside County Fair and National Date Festival. He tries spicy treats like the fire fingers kartwheel and the sonoran fire dog.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Eggs Men
programme.desc: Noah Cappe gets the royal treatment with "ye olde eats" at the Hoggetown Medieval Faire in Gainesville, Fla. Meat is all over the menu, and Noah goes wild for the smoked bison burger and the wild boar leg.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Mad Snacks: Berry Road
programme.desc: Noah Cappe travels back in time at the Texas Renaissance Festival, where the medieval-inspired meals range from the Sir Francis fry dog to the crown jewel. Then it's over to Plant City, Fla., where Noah chows down on some berry delicious carnival treats.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Requiem For An Ice Cream
programme.desc: Noah Cappe rolls into Alabama for some country carnival cookin' at the Greater Gulf State Fair in Mobile. He digs into the dirty bird turkey leg and takes a ride on a crazy midway mashup.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Manchester, New Hampshire
programme.desc: Casey Webb's tasty travels bring him to Manchester, New Hampshire, for an epic portion of barbecue that's served on a shovel at KC's Rib Shack. At local favourite Mr. Mac's Mac and Cheese, he only has eyes for their magnum opus.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Alaska
programme.desc: Casey's nationwide feast brings him to Alaska, where he cracks into crab legs fit for a king at Slippery Salmon Bar and Grill in Anchorage. In Talkeetna, he samples the local wild game, ordering a slab of reindeer meatloaf at Denali BrewPub.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Portland, OR
programme.desc: Casey heads to Portland, Oregon, to taste mouth-watering Peruvian chicken, a succulent porchetta sandwich and a two-pound burrito packed with ten of the spiciest peppers in the world.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Chicago, IL
programme.desc: Casey travels to Chicago for some epic Windy City eats, including a massive triple-threat pork sandwich and a challenge featuring a two-pound double bacon cheeseburger and two pounds of 'Irish Nacho' cheese fries.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Key Ingredient
programme.desc: Alton Brown reveals the surprise ingredient that goes into the best cinnamon roll ever, Alex Guarnaschelli loves sourdough bread made with pickle juice, and Monti Carlo heads to New Orleans for the most unique, irresistible oysters ever.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: New Deli Done Right
programme.desc: America's best delicatessens are serving everything from perfected tried-and-true classics to inventive new spins on old favourites. Wolfgang Puck shares the best hot smoked salmon in the country.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Kids' Menu

programme.title: NFL 360: Fritz Pollard: A Forgotten Man
programme.desc: NFL 360 pays tribute to Fritz Pollard, one of pro football's first black players and its first black head coach. Pollard integrated the fledgling NFL a century ago, winning its inaugural title before a wave of segregation cast him aside.

programme.title: A Football Life: Walter Payton

programme.title: A Football Life: Doug Williams
programme.desc: Doug Williams grew up with no running water in rural Louisiana during an era when few African Americans played quarterback. He would overcome adversity and prejudice to become a Heisman nominee, first round pick and Super Bowl MVP.

programme.title: Batwoman: Drinke Me
